query: When does the semester start at the University of Vienna?

retrieved.title: Studying at the University of Vienna again – different degree programme
heading_path: Different master programme with selection procedure
Deadlines
retrieved.text:
Application and admission period for the academic year 2026/27:

- from 2 March to 7 April 2026

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Admission period: from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

retrieved.title: Studying at the University of Vienna again – different degree programme
heading_path: Different master programme without selection procedure
Note for graduates of the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
The application and admission period for graduates of the University of Vienna who want to study an eligible master programme according to the curriculum for winter semester 2026/27 is from 22 June to 31 October 2026. The application and admission period for summer semester 2027 is from 16 November 2026 to 31 March 2027.

The direct admission to [consecutive master programmes in the same discipline](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/masterstudien/mit-abschluss-der-universitaet-wien/tagesaktuelle-zulassung/) (only available in German) is possible year round for graduates of a bachelor programme at the University of Vienna.

- Admission period: after the announcement of results to 31 October 2026

Summer semester 2027:

- Admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

The application and admission period for summer semester 2027 is from 16 November 2026 to 31 March 2027.

The direct admission to [consecutive master programmes in the same discipline](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/masterstudien/mit-abschluss-der-universitaet-wien/tagesaktuelle-zulassung/) (only available in German) is possible year round for graduates of a bachelor programme at the University of Vienna.

retrieved.title: The academic year
retrieved.text:
The academic year is divided into winter semester and summer semester. Each semester consists of a period during which courses are held and a lecuture-free period ("holidays").

- The University of Vienna is closed on [public holidays](https://www.wien.gv.at/amtshelfer/feiertage/).
- [Application and admission periods for degree programmes without an entrance exam procedure](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/)
- [Application and admission period for degree programmes with an entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/deadlines-for-entrance-exam-procedures/)

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Beginning of semester and classes | 1 March 2026 |
| Rectors's Day (no classes) | 12 March 2026 |
| Easter break | 30 March to 12 April 2026 |
| Whitsun break | 23 May to 25 May 2026 |
| End of classes | 30 June 2026 |
| Lecture-free period (summer break) | 1 July to 30 September 2026 |
| End of semester | 30 September 2026 |

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Beginning of semester and classes | 1 October 2026 |
| All Souls Day | 2 November 2026 |
| Christmas break | 21 December 2026 to 6 January 2027 |
| End of classes | 31 January 2027 |
| Lecture-free period (semester break) | 1 to 28 February 2027 |
| End of semester | 28 February 2027 |

The application and admission period for graduates of the University of Vienna who want to study an eligible master programme according to the curriculum for winter semester 2026/27 is from 22 June to 31 October 2026. The application and admission period for summer semester 2027 is from 16 November 2026 to 31 March 2027.

The direct admission to [consecutive master programmes in the same discipline](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/masterstudien/mit-abschluss-der-universitaet-wien/tagesaktuelle-zulassung/) (only available in German) is possible year round for graduates of a bachelor programme at the University of Vienna.

retrieved.title: Attending courses out of interest
retrieved.text:
You are interested in individual courses at the University of Vienna and want to further your education without completing a degree programme? Then a non-degree programme is the right choice.

The information on this website applies to persons who are admitted at the University of Vienna for the first time. If you were admitted to a degree or non-degree programme at the University of Vienna in the past, please read the information on the [resumption of studies](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/).

You need the following document as JPEG or PNG file:

- your valid passport or personal ID card (front and back)

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Application and admission period: from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Application and admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

retrieved.title: Planning your semester
retrieved.text:
At the University of Vienna, you do not get a ready-made schedule, telling you what courses to complete each semester. Rather, you have to plan your semester yourself.
The most important tools for doing so are the student platform u:space and the course directory u:find.

[Information about u:space and u:find](/en/web-services-uspace-ufind-moodle-webmail/)

#

retrieved.title: Application and admission periods
retrieved.text:
[### Attention: changes for bachelor and diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure for the academic year 2026/27 (please click here)](https://aufnahmeverfahren.univie.ac.at/en/)

On this site you will find an overview of the application and admission periods for bachelor/diploma and master programmes at the University of Vienna.

- You have to submit your application for admission to a degree programme within the [application period (= general admission period)](#c955669).
- You can complete the admission procedure within the [admission period (= registration period for the continuation of studies)](#c955681).

The winter semester always starts on 1 October, the summer semester on 1 March.
[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

- The application for degree programmes with entrance exam or selection procedure is only possible once per academic year!
  - [Bachelor/diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/)
  - [Master programmes with selection procedure](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/#c876069)
- Separate application and admission periods apply for admission to [non-degree programmes](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/).
- If you want to attend the [Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/) you also have to keep the registration periods of the VWU in mind.

retrieved.title: Leave of absence
retrieved.text:
A leave of absence is only possible for precisely defined reasons. You cannot apply for a leave of absence for an individual programme. It affects all your studies at the University of Vienna.

Please read the consequences of a leave of absence on your studies carefully. Afterwards you can decide whether you want to apply for a leave of absence or not.

Submit the application before the start of the semester within the relevant deadline. During the semester, a leave of absence is only possible for specific reasons. You will also need additional confirmation.

If you are attending a university course, please contact the [ServiceCenter of the Postgraduate Center](https://postgraduatecenter.at/en/about-us/contact/). Doctoral/PhD students should contact [doktorat.beurlaubung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,fqmvqtcv0dgwtncwdwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).

- Winter semester 2026/27: from 13 July to 30 September 2026
- Summer semester 2027: from 11 January to 28 February 2027

- Summer semester 2026: from 1 March to 30 September 2026
- Winter semester 2026/27: from 1 October 2026 to 28 February 2027
- Summer semester 2027: from 1 March to 30 September 2027

The leave of absence exempts you from the tuition fee, but not from the Student's Union fee. Please pay this fee in due time. Otherwise, your studies at the University of Vienna will be automatically closed.

retrieved.title: Co-registration at the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
Are you studying at another Austrian university, but would like to complete individual courses at the University of Vienna? You can have these courses recognised at your home university by applying for co-registration. Don't forget to register for courses after your application has been approved.

Please note:

- You cannot complete a degree programme at the University of Vienna as a co-registered student.
- The co-registration has to be applied for each semester.
- If you are enrolled in a Doctoral/PhD programme and want to co-register, please contact the [Center for Doctoral Studies](https://doktorat.univie.ac.at/en/doctoralphd-programmes/co-registration/).

If you are studying at the University of Vienna and would like to take courses at another university, please contact the [StudyServiceCentre (SSC) or the StudyServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of your degree programme.

You need the following documents as PDF files:

- [the pre-approval form (PDF)](/fileadmin/user_upload/p_studieren/Studienzulassung/Formulare/Pre-approval_of_the_co-registration.pdf)
- the student record sheet of the current semester (the semester, in which you want to co-register)
- your student ID card, valid passport or personal ID

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Application period: from 13 July to 31 Oktober 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Application period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

retrieved.title: Deadlines and dates
retrieved.text:
[Entrance exam procedure](/en/)

# Deadlines and dates

Are you interested in a bachelor's/diploma programme with an entrance exam/aptitude test procedure? On this page, you will find all the information you need about our application and admission deadlines and test dates for the academic year 2026/27.

- ### Application period

  The application period is the time frame during which you can apply to study at the University of Vienna by submitting your application for admission.

  During the application period, you can apply for the following: first-time admission, change of programme, admission to an additional programme, and resumption of studies.

  Our tip: Apply right at the beginning of the application period! This will allow you to complete the admission process quickly after the admission period begins.
- ### Admission period

  During the admission period, you complete all the necessary steps in the admission process and are officially admitted.

  Please note: This is only possible if you have applied within the application period and submitted an application for admission to your desired degree programme. You must also meet all admission requirements.

  Our tip: Complete the admission procedure as early as possible. That way, you will get off to a good start in your studies!

retrieved.title: Our offers and projects
heading_path: School meets Uni - Offers for school classes
retrieved.text:
The Vienna University Library is open to all high school students free of charge. It supports students with their research for the ABA (formerly VWA) or the BHS diploma thesis. School classes can attend library courses free of charge. The students get to know the university library. They learn how to get a library card and how to borrow books. They are also introduced to research strategies using the central media search engine u:search.

- [Library courses for school classes](https://bibliothek.univie.ac.at/schuelerinnen-schulungen.html)

If you are interested, you can also book a guided tour through the Main Building of the University of Vienna. The guided tour through the Main Building cost €35,-- per school class. A maximum of 30 people per group can participate in the tour. To arrange a tour, please send an email to fuehrungen@univie.ac.at. A combination of guided tour and lecture is the best way to get to know the University of Vienna.

© Universität Wien

retrieved.title: Minimum number of credits
heading_path: FAQs about the minimum number of credits
retrieved.text: degree programme expires on 31 October or 31 March, depending on whether the fourth semester is the summer or the winter semester. You will be excluded from this programme for four semesters. Once four semesters have passed, you can apply for re-admission to the same degree programme at the University of Vienna. - What options do I have if it becomes apparent that I will not be able to achieve the minimum number of credits? Option 1: Try everything you can and make use of the following offers and information: - [Registration for courses and exams](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/semesterplanung/anmeldung-zu-lehrveranstaltungenpruefungen/) - [Support services provided by the University of Vienna for successful studying](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/en/services-for-students/) - [Psychological Counselling Service for students: counselling for study-related and personal issues](https://www.studentenberatung.at/) - [Information for students with physical/mental impairments](/en/accessible-studies/) - [Student life - Who can help me with...?](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/) Option 2: If you can claim one of the precisely defined reasons, apply for a leave of absence. Please note the application deadlines, as well as the consequences of taking a leave of absence. [All information about taking a leave of absence](/en/admission/leave-of-absence/) Option 3: You do not want to continue your studies? Close your degree programme. Before closing, inform yourself about the possible implications for re-enrolment. [All information about closing a degree programme/withdrawing from the University of Vienna](/en/admission/closing-a-degree-programme/) - Where can I check the ECTS credits obtained for my studies? For each ongoing degree

retrieved.title: Welcome at the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
How is my degree programme structured? What are the next steps? How does semester planning and the exams in the introductory and orientation period (STEOP) work? These questions will be answered at the orientation courses (OV) for your degree programme. At many locations, there are additional welcome events alongside the OV. **The programme is constantly being updated**, so do check back from time to time!

[Discover the orientation course!](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/search.html?x_c=c%3A24&filter=courses&query=l%3Aov%20l%3Ala-ov%20%2Bct%20c%3A100)

©
Ian Ehm

Previous

©

Next

Get to know the main building, find out about studying abroad or discover the library’s services. Workshops, guided tours, and other information events starting in mid-September offer skills and tips for your studies.

New events are being added continuously.

retrieved.title: Entrance exam procedure
heading_path: Your path to a study place in 2026/27
retrieved.text:
- ### Step 1

  **From now on**

  Find out more:

  - [What is an entrance exam/aptitude test procedure?](#c4427)
  - [Does my preferred programme have an entrance exam/aptitude test procedure?](#c7588)
  - [How do I find my dream degree programme?](#c4476)
  - [What will change in 2026/27?](https://aufnahmeverfahren.univie.ac.at/en/news/detail/entrance-exam-procedure-2026-27)
- ### Step 2

  **Deadline: 2 March to 4 May 2026**

  The application period has ended. There is no extension period. You can only take part in this year’s entrance exam/aptitude test procedure if you applied within the application period.

  Please note: The entrance exam and aptitude test procedure only take place once per academic year!
- ### Step 3

  **Written tests: From 6 July 2026 onwards**

  The written tests take place on site in Vienna. We will inform you until the end of this year about the exact test dates. There are no alternative dates. Therefore, make sure to keep the test day free!

  Note: The entrance exam and aptitude test procedures of the University of Vienna only take place once per academic year!
- ### Step 4

  **Results: Around 2 weeks after the test**

  We will send your test result via e-mail. If you were successful, you will receive info about your further steps for your admission and starting your studies.

  The winter semester always starts on 1 October.

The video has been loaded and can be found below on the page. For an English translation you can turn on the English subtitles. Previous © Next - [## Orientation courses At the orientation courses (OV), you’ll find out everything you need to know about your degree programme.](#c8132) - [## Workshops and guided tours Get to know the university and take a guided tour of the main building. Or take part one of our exciting workshops.](#c22912) - [## unileben At the start of the semester, the unileben education fair invites all students to the Main Building. You’ll find

reranked.title: Planning your semester
reranked.text:
At the University of Vienna, you do not get a ready-made schedule, telling you what courses to complete each semester. Rather, you have to plan your semester yourself.
The most important tools for doing so are the student platform u:space and the course directory u:find.

[Information about u:space and u:find](/en/web-services-uspace-ufind-moodle-webmail/)

#

reranked.title: Application and admission periods
reranked.text:
[### Attention: changes for bachelor and diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure for the academic year 2026/27 (please click here)](https://aufnahmeverfahren.univie.ac.at/en/)

On this site you will find an overview of the application and admission periods for bachelor/diploma and master programmes at the University of Vienna.

- You have to submit your application for admission to a degree programme within the [application period (= general admission period)](#c955669).
- You can complete the admission procedure within the [admission period (= registration period for the continuation of studies)](#c955681).

The winter semester always starts on 1 October, the summer semester on 1 March.
[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

- The application for degree programmes with entrance exam or selection procedure is only possible once per academic year!
  - [Bachelor/diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/)
  - [Master programmes with selection procedure](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/#c876069)
- Separate application and admission periods apply for admission to [non-degree programmes](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/).
- If you want to attend the [Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/) you also have to keep the registration periods of the VWU in mind.

reranked.title: Leave of absence
reranked.text:
A leave of absence is only possible for precisely defined reasons. You cannot apply for a leave of absence for an individual programme. It affects all your studies at the University of Vienna.

Please read the consequences of a leave of absence on your studies carefully. Afterwards you can decide whether you want to apply for a leave of absence or not.

Submit the application before the start of the semester within the relevant deadline. During the semester, a leave of absence is only possible for specific reasons. You will also need additional confirmation.

If you are attending a university course, please contact the [ServiceCenter of the Postgraduate Center](https://postgraduatecenter.at/en/about-us/contact/). Doctoral/PhD students should contact [doktorat.beurlaubung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,fqmvqtcv0dgwtncwdwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).

- Winter semester 2026/27: from 13 July to 30 September 2026
- Summer semester 2027: from 11 January to 28 February 2027

- Summer semester 2026: from 1 March to 30 September 2026
- Winter semester 2026/27: from 1 October 2026 to 28 February 2027
- Summer semester 2027: from 1 March to 30 September 2027

The leave of absence exempts you from the tuition fee, but not from the Student's Union fee. Please pay this fee in due time. Otherwise, your studies at the University of Vienna will be automatically closed.

reranked.title: Co-registration at the University of Vienna
reranked.text:
Are you studying at another Austrian university, but would like to complete individual courses at the University of Vienna? You can have these courses recognised at your home university by applying for co-registration. Don't forget to register for courses after your application has been approved.

Please note:

- You cannot complete a degree programme at the University of Vienna as a co-registered student.
- The co-registration has to be applied for each semester.
- If you are enrolled in a Doctoral/PhD programme and want to co-register, please contact the [Center for Doctoral Studies](https://doktorat.univie.ac.at/en/doctoralphd-programmes/co-registration/).

If you are studying at the University of Vienna and would like to take courses at another university, please contact the [StudyServiceCentre (SSC) or the StudyServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of your degree programme.

You need the following documents as PDF files:

- [the pre-approval form (PDF)](/fileadmin/user_upload/p_studieren/Studienzulassung/Formulare/Pre-approval_of_the_co-registration.pdf)
- the student record sheet of the current semester (the semester, in which you want to co-register)
- your student ID card, valid passport or personal ID

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Application period: from 13 July to 31 Oktober 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Application period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

reranked.title: Deadlines and dates
reranked.text:
[Entrance exam procedure](/en/)

# Deadlines and dates

Are you interested in a bachelor's/diploma programme with an entrance exam/aptitude test procedure? On this page, you will find all the information you need about our application and admission deadlines and test dates for the academic year 2026/27.

- ### Application period

  The application period is the time frame during which you can apply to study at the University of Vienna by submitting your application for admission.

  During the application period, you can apply for the following: first-time admission, change of programme, admission to an additional programme, and resumption of studies.

  Our tip: Apply right at the beginning of the application period! This will allow you to complete the admission process quickly after the admission period begins.
- ### Admission period

  During the admission period, you complete all the necessary steps in the admission process and are officially admitted.

  Please note: This is only possible if you have applied within the application period and submitted an application for admission to your desired degree programme. You must also meet all admission requirements.

  Our tip: Complete the admission procedure as early as possible. That way, you will get off to a good start in your studies!

reranked.title: Our offers and projects
heading_path: School meets Uni - Offers for school classes
reranked.text:
The Vienna University Library is open to all high school students free of charge. It supports students with their research for the ABA (formerly VWA) or the BHS diploma thesis. School classes can attend library courses free of charge. The students get to know the university library. They learn how to get a library card and how to borrow books. They are also introduced to research strategies using the central media search engine u:search.

- [Library courses for school classes](https://bibliothek.univie.ac.at/schuelerinnen-schulungen.html)

If you are interested, you can also book a guided tour through the Main Building of the University of Vienna. The guided tour through the Main Building cost €35,-- per school class. A maximum of 30 people per group can participate in the tour. To arrange a tour, please send an email to fuehrungen@univie.ac.at. A combination of guided tour and lecture is the best way to get to know the University of Vienna.

© Universität Wien

reranked.title: Minimum number of credits
heading_path: FAQs about the minimum number of credits
reranked.text: degree programme expires on 31 October or 31 March, depending on whether the fourth semester is the summer or the winter semester. You will be excluded from this programme for four semesters. Once four semesters have passed, you can apply for re-admission to the same degree programme at the University of Vienna. - What options do I have if it becomes apparent that I will not be able to achieve the minimum number of credits? Option 1: Try everything you can and make use of the following offers and information: - [Registration for courses and exams](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/semesterplanung/anmeldung-zu-lehrveranstaltungenpruefungen/) - [Support services provided by the University of Vienna for successful studying](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/en/services-for-students/) - [Psychological Counselling Service for students: counselling for study-related and personal issues](https://www.studentenberatung.at/) - [Information for students with physical/mental impairments](/en/accessible-studies/) - [Student life - Who can help me with...?](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/) Option 2: If you can claim one of the precisely defined reasons, apply for a leave of absence. Please note the application deadlines, as well as the consequences of taking a leave of absence. [All information about taking a leave of absence](/en/admission/leave-of-absence/) Option 3: You do not want to continue your studies? Close your degree programme. Before closing, inform yourself about the possible implications for re-enrolment. [All information about closing a degree programme/withdrawing from the University of Vienna](/en/admission/closing-a-degree-programme/) - Where can I check the ECTS credits obtained for my studies? For each ongoing degree

reranked.title: Welcome at the University of Vienna
reranked.text:
How is my degree programme structured? What are the next steps? How does semester planning and the exams in the introductory and orientation period (STEOP) work? These questions will be answered at the orientation courses (OV) for your degree programme. At many locations, there are additional welcome events alongside the OV. **The programme is constantly being updated**, so do check back from time to time!

[Discover the orientation course!](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/search.html?x_c=c%3A24&filter=courses&query=l%3Aov%20l%3Ala-ov%20%2Bct%20c%3A100)

©
Ian Ehm

Previous

©

Next

Get to know the main building, find out about studying abroad or discover the library’s services. Workshops, guided tours, and other information events starting in mid-September offer skills and tips for your studies.

New events are being added continuously.

reranked.title: Entrance exam procedure
heading_path: Your path to a study place in 2026/27
reranked.text:
- ### Step 1

  **From now on**

  Find out more:

  - [What is an entrance exam/aptitude test procedure?](#c4427)
  - [Does my preferred programme have an entrance exam/aptitude test procedure?](#c7588)
  - [How do I find my dream degree programme?](#c4476)
  - [What will change in 2026/27?](https://aufnahmeverfahren.univie.ac.at/en/news/detail/entrance-exam-procedure-2026-27)
- ### Step 2

  **Deadline: 2 March to 4 May 2026**

  The application period has ended. There is no extension period. You can only take part in this year’s entrance exam/aptitude test procedure if you applied within the application period.

  Please note: The entrance exam and aptitude test procedure only take place once per academic year!
- ### Step 3

  **Written tests: From 6 July 2026 onwards**

  The written tests take place on site in Vienna. We will inform you until the end of this year about the exact test dates. There are no alternative dates. Therefore, make sure to keep the test day free!

  Note: The entrance exam and aptitude test procedures of the University of Vienna only take place once per academic year!
- ### Step 4

  **Results: Around 2 weeks after the test**

  We will send your test result via e-mail. If you were successful, you will receive info about your further steps for your admission and starting your studies.

  The winter semester always starts on 1 October.

The application and admission period for graduates of the University of Vienna who want to study an eligible master programme according to the curriculum for winter semester 2026/27 is from 22 June to 31 October 2026. The direct admission to [consecutive master programmes in the same discipline](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/masterstudien/mit-abschluss-der-universitaet-wien/tagesaktuelle-zulassung/) (only available in German) is possible year round for graduates of a bachelor programme at the University of Vienna.

selected.text:
The academic year is divided into winter semester and summer semester. Each semester consists of a period during which courses are held and a lecuture-free period ("holidays").

prompt_messages.content:
You are a concise assistant for prospective and current University of Vienna students. Answer the question using ONLY the numbered sources provided.
Rules:
- Base every statement on the sources; never add outside knowledge or guess.
- Cite each claim inline with the bracket number(s) of the source(s) you used, e.g. [1] or [2][3]. Cite only sources you actually relied on.
- If the sources do not contain the answer, reply that you don't know, and do NOT cite any sources.
- Keep the answer short and direct (it may be read aloud by a voice assistant).

Sources:
[1] Studying at the University of Vienna again – different degree programme — Different master programme with selection procedure > Deadlines

| Lecture-free period (summer break) | 1 July to 30 September 2026 |
| End of semester | 30 September 2026 |

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Beginning of semester and classes | 1 October 2026 |
| All Souls Day | 2 November 2026 |
| Christmas break | 21 December 2026 to 6 January 2027 |
| End of classes | 31 January 2027 |
| Lecture-free period (semester break) | 1 to 28 February 2027 |
| End of semester | 28 February 2027 |

[6] Change of master programme — Master programmes without entrance exam procedure > Note for graduates of the University of Vienna
The application and admission period for graduates of the University of Vienna who want to study an eligible master programme according to the curriculum for winter semester 2026/27 is from 22 June to 31 October 2026. The application and admission period for summer semester 2027 is from 16 November 2026 to 31 March 2027.

The direct admission to [consecutive master programmes in the same discipline](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/masterstudien/mit-abschluss-der-universitaet-wien/tagesaktuelle-zulassung/) (only available in German) is possible year round for graduates of a bachelor programme at the University of Vienna.

Question: When does the semester start at the University of Vienna?

generate.raw_response: The semester starts at the University of Vienna on 1 October for the winter semester [1][3] and on 1 March for the summer semester [1][3].

answer: The semester starts at the University of Vienna on 1 October for the winter semester [1][3] and on 1 March for the summer semester [1][3].
